message:
fix(search/query): include per-client instance id in cache_namespace so concurrent SearchClient instances don't share federated readers

Follow-up to commit bd493536 (federated search reader). The
cache_namespace was originally built from `CACHE_KEY_VERSION + schema
hash + index_path`, which is deterministic across SearchClient
instances constructed against the same index. That means two
SearchClient instances in the same process — a legitimate pattern
when e.g. one is held by the TUI and another by a background warm
job — would map to the same entry in `FEDERATED_SEARCH_READERS`.

Two concrete bugs fall out of that:
1. When the first client is dropped, its `Drop for SearchClient`
   impl calls `FEDERATED_SEARCH_READERS.write().remove(&cache_namespace)`
   and evicts the readers that are still in use by the other live
   client. The surviving client's cache_namespace lookup then
   returns `None` mid-query.
2. A client constructed while another is still warming the readers
   will see a half-populated reader Vec (because Arc cloning out of
   the map returns the Arc *as it was at lookup time*, not as it
   grows later).

src/search/query.rs
- New process-wide counter
  `static SEARCH_CLIENT_INSTANCE_COUNTER: AtomicU64 = AtomicU64::new(1)`.
- During SearchClient construction, bump the counter via
  `SEARCH_CLIENT_INSTANCE_COUNTER.fetch_add(1, Ordering::Relaxed)`
  and include the resulting `client:{id}` segment in the
  cache_namespace:
  `v{CACHE_KEY_VERSION}|schema:{FS_CASS_SCHEMA_HASH}|client:{id}|index:{path}`.
- No other logic changes. The Drop impl continues to remove the
  client's own namespace entry (now guaranteed unique), and any
  other live client keeps its own Arc<Vec<FederatedIndexReader>>
  intact because the key is different.

Relaxed ordering is sufficient: the counter is monotonic and its
only consumer is uniqueness, not ordering of operations.
